Home / Services / Garage Floor Coatings

Garage Floor Coatings

Salt-resistant garage floors that withstand plow blades and -30°F winters.

Minnesota garages endure more abuse than almost any other space - from snowplow blades scraping across the surface to road salt eating through inferior coatings. Our 4-layer system uses military-grade polymer blends with rubberized undercoats that flex during temperature swings.

We start by pressure washing and diamond-grinding your slab to remove all traces of winter damage. After repairing cracks with thermal expansion joints, we apply our proprietary primer that bonds at a molecular level. The final quartz-infused topcoat resists tire marks from winter studs while providing traction during icy months.

Popular options include our 'Lakeshore Slate' flake blend that hides salt stains or our 'Mill City Metallic' series with copper accents inspired by the Stone Arch Bridge.

Our Process

  1. 01 Step 1: Diagnostic inspection checking for freeze-thaw damage
  2. 02 Step 2: Mechanical preparation with 40-grit diamond grinders
  3. 03 Step 3: Hot water pressure wash (critical for salt removal)
  4. 04 Step 4: Custom mix application with temperature-adjusted resins

Benefits

  • Eliminates tire marks from winter studs
  • Withstands -30°F without cracking
  • Slip-resistant texture for snowy boots
  • 10-year warranty against salt damage

Frequently Asked Questions

How soon after application can I park my car?

Our fast-cure formula allows light vehicle traffic in 24 hours, even in winter. Full cure (including RV parking) reaches maximum hardness in 72 hours.

Will it peel when I chip ice off my bumper?

We use impact-resistant topcoats rated for 18,000 PSI - that's stronger than most commercial parking ramps. Ice chunks will bounce right off.

Get a Free Estimate

Ready to get started with garage floor coatings? Contact us today.
